job_statement: Monitor Argo CD instance availability and component health
context: As a cluster administrator managing GitOps deployments, when I need to ensure Argo CD instances remain operational, I want to monitor the availability of Argo CD custom resource workloads and receive alerts when components are unhealthy, so I can proactively respond to issues before they impact continuous delivery pipelines.
pain_point: Lack of visibility into Argo CD component health can lead to undetected failures in application-controller, repo-server, or server workloads, causing deployment delays or failures without administrator awareness
evidence: monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc -> Abstract section, lines 9-12 [assembly: monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc, type: ASSEMBLY]
notes: Core observability capability for production GitOps operations. Requires monitoring stack and kube-state-metrics prerequisites. Source assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc

job_statement: Enable monitoring for specific Argo CD instances
context: As a cluster administrator setting up GitOps monitoring, when I need to track the health of a particular Argo CD instance, I want to enable monitoring by configuring the Argo CD custom resource, so the Operator creates PrometheusRule objects with alert rules for all managed workloads.
pain_point: Without enabling monitoring, there is no automated alerting when replica counts drift from desired state, requiring manual checks to detect component failures
evidence: gitops-enabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc -> Full module, lines 1-65 [module: modules/gitops-enabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Procedural task. Sets .spec.monitoring.enabled to true in ArgoCD CR. Creates PrometheusRule with alert rules. Source module: modules/gitops-enabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc (PROCEDURE)

job_statement: Verify PrometheusRule alert configuration after enabling monitoring
context: As a cluster administrator who just enabled monitoring, when I need to confirm that alerts are properly configured, I want to verify the PrometheusRule contains expected alert rules for Argo CD component workloads, so I can ensure monitoring is working correctly before relying on it.
pain_point: Uncertainty about whether monitoring was successfully enabled could lead to false confidence in alert coverage
evidence: gitops-enabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc -> Procedure step 2, lines 34-64 [module: modules/gitops-enabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Verification step. Checks for alerts like ApplicationSetControllerNotReady in the created PrometheusRule. Source module: modules/gitops-enabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc (PROCEDURE)

job_statement: Disable monitoring for Argo CD instances
context: As a cluster administrator managing Argo CD instances, when I no longer need monitoring for a specific instance (e.g., decommissioning, cost reduction, or moving to different monitoring solution), I want to disable monitoring by updating the Argo CD custom resource, so the PrometheusRule is automatically deleted and alert noise is reduced.
pain_point: Leaving monitoring enabled for unused instances creates unnecessary PrometheusRule objects and potential alert fatigue
evidence: gitops-disabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc -> Full module, lines 1-30 [module: modules/gitops-disabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Procedural task. Sets .spec.monitoring.enabled to false in ArgoCD CR. Deletes the created PrometheusRule. Source module: modules/gitops-disabling-monitoring-for-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc (PROCEDURE)

job_statement: Set up monitoring prerequisites for Argo CD workload monitoring
context: As a cluster administrator preparing to enable Argo CD monitoring, when I need to ensure the monitoring infrastructure is in place, I want to verify cluster-admin access, monitoring stack configuration, kube-state-metrics availability, and user workload monitoring settings, so monitoring can function correctly when enabled.
pain_point: Missing prerequisites like kube-state-metrics or improper monitoring stack configuration will cause monitoring enablement to fail or produce no alerts
evidence: monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc -> Prerequisites section, lines 14-27 [assembly: monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc, type: ASSEMBLY]
notes: Prerequisites include cluster-admin role, GitOps installed, monitoring stack in openshift-monitoring, kube-state-metrics service, and optional user workload monitoring for non-openshift namespaces. Source assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc

job_statement: Enable user workload monitoring for Argo CD instances in custom namespaces
context: As a cluster administrator deploying Argo CD outside openshift-managed namespaces, when I need to monitor an Argo CD instance in a user-defined project, I want to enable user workload monitoring cluster-wide, so the monitoring stack picks up the PrometheusRule created by the GitOps Operator.
pain_point: Without user workload monitoring enabled, PrometheusRules in non-openshift namespaces are ignored, resulting in no alerts despite monitoring being configured
evidence: monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc -> Prerequisites note, lines 24-27 [assembly: monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc, type: ASSEMBLY]
notes: Critical for monitoring Argo CD instances in namespaces not starting with 'openshift-*'. Requires enabling monitoring for user-defined projects cluster-wide. Source assembly: cicd/gitops/monitoring-argo-cd-custom-resource-workloads.adoc
